public_reputation: Known locally through day-to-day work as night-lantern inspector.
appearance: Practical layers, inspection tools, and a habit of carrying evidence or notes keep inquiry visibly close at hand.
manner: Asks more than they answer, preserving doubt until the evidence can bear weight. In The Union, that reads as someone deal-aware, balanced, and procedurally diplomatic, with an eye on leverage and precedent, and the habits of a night-lantern inspector still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of night-lantern inspector in and around Craftspire, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Keep Craftspire workable through the obligations of night-lantern inspector while managing the immediate pressure of institutional scrutiny in The Union.
hidden_pressure: Must keep suspicion disciplined so that inquiry does not collapse into factional theater. Around Craftspire, that usually means carrying institutional scrutiny, reputation risk, daily strain without letting the role of night-lantern inspector visibly slip.

adventure_hooks.summary: Working with Tess Ledgermere can place the party inside an active inquiry where every answer creates new exposure.
Tess Ledgermere can share a strong lead if the party retrieves testimony, material proof, or a missing intermediary.

support_scope_notes: Promoted from a preexisting named interface into the unified full-content NPC architecture. Sparse unsupported fields remain intentionally restrained. Appearance extended under V28_8 from role/locality/public-signal derivation rather than decorative fabrication.
public_signal: Questioning silence, inspection habits, and careful note-taking make Tess Ledgermere look like inquiry before accusation.

encounter_context.usual_environment: Craftspire: inquiry rooms, witness routes, guarded evidence sites, or contested scenes
who_seeks_them_out: those needing a problem solved quietly
